symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive chest tightness and dry cough in an established asthmatic

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chest tightness and cough are core exacerbation symptoms in known asthma.

symptoms.symptom_name: Failing short-acting bronchodilator response (every 2 hours, diminishing effect)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Escalating SABA use with a falling response marks a severe, steroid-requiring exacerbation and is a first-aid call-000 trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: Speaking in short phrases / breathless at minimal exertion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Speech limited to short phrases is a validated severity marker of acute asthma.

symptoms.symptom_name: Audible polyphonic wheeze on the video call

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Audible wheeze is video-observable and patient-obtainable; supports airflow obstruction, though it does not quantify severity (a silent chest is more ominous than a loud wheeze).

symptoms.symptom_name: Nocturnal awakenings from cough/wheeze x3 nights

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Night-time symptoms indicate recent loss of control preceding the acute flare.
Under-treated airway inflammation from irregular ICS use predisposes to exacerbations and is the key modifiable factor.
Identifiable aeroallergen trigger consistent with an allergic asthma flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged expiratory phase with widespread polyphonic expiratory wheeze on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Auscultatory hallmark of diffuse bronchospasm — sealed clinician finding, not obtainable via telehealth.
Polyphonic wheeze with clear bases argues against pulmonary oedema.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity is under-quantified; PEF is not obtainable in this telehealth encounter (no home device reported).

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PEF 40-60% predicted defines a moderate-to-severe exacerbation; measured by the clinician, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: SpO2 94% on room air with RR 24 and HR 108

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective hypoxia and tachypnoea/tachycardia — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers severity from patient-reportable markers, not a measured SpO2.
Absence of fever and infective prodrome argues against an infective cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: No pleuritic chest pain and no calf swelling/DVT features

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of pleuritic pain and unilateral leg findings lowers PE probability, though it never fully excludes it clinically.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Failing reliever + short-phrase speech + PEF ~55% + hypoxia together define a moderate-to-severe exacerbation needing urgent hands-on care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person peak expiratory flow and pulse oximetry
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Objectively grades severity and response to bronchodilator; only obtainable at an in-person/emergency assessment.
Confirms diffuse bronchospasm and detects the ominous silent chest; not possible via telehealth.
